title: senecio anapetes
intro: Senecio anapetes is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to various parts of the world, including New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including hepatotoxic and hepatocarcinogenic effects, which are associated with the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. However, its roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, although its use is not recommended due to the potential risks associated with its toxic compounds.
